title: Authors and Citation
text: Kalyn Dorheim. Maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: c, person, person) (2022). MEMC: Microbial Explicit Model Comparison: R package supporting model exploration. R package version 0.1.0, https://microbial-explicit-model.github.io/MEMC.
code: @Manual{,   title = {MEMC: Microbial Explicit Model Comparison: a R package supporting model exploration},   author = {{c} and {person} and {person)}},   year = {2022},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://microbial-explicit-model.github.io/MEMC}, }

title: Microbial Explicit Model Comparison: a R package supporting model exploration
text: MEMC R package representation different microbial explicit carbon models flexible framework model comparison exercises.

title: Microbial Explicit Model Comparison: a R package supporting model exploration
text: Follow download installation instructions R R studio.

title: Microbial Explicit Model Comparison: a R package supporting model exploration
text: Use remotes install MEMC built R package directory github. install build specific branch run following.
code: # use install.packages("remotes") to install this package the first time. library(remotes)  # Now build and install the R package on your local machine. install_github('Microbial-Explicit-Model/MEMC') install_github("Microbial-Explicit-Model/MEMC@branch_name")

title: Microbial Explicit Model Comparison: a R package supporting model exploration
text: Clone repository install package development mode using devtools::load_all() call R studio root directory. Please refer r-pkgs information build develop R packages. making changes code, checking new git branch, pulling new commits need clean rebuild MEMC package. Rstudio click Build > Clean Rebuild run following R console.

what: MEMC model configurations for MEND Wang et al. 2013 — COMISSION_model

what: Set up a model configuration — configure_model
title: Set up a model configuration — configure_model
text: Set model configuration

title: Set up a model configuration — configure_model
code: configure_model(   params,   state,   name = "unnamed",   DOMdecomp = "MM",   POMdecomp = "MM",   MBdecay = "DD" )

title: Set up a model configuration — configure_model
text: params data.table containing following columns: parameter, value, units. state vector initial state values, must named name string name model configuration, default set "MEND". DOMdecomp string indicating dynamics used model microbial decomposition DOM, one  following "MM", "RMM", "ECA" POMdecomp string indicating dynamics used model microbial decomposition POM, one  following "MM", "RMM", "ECA", "LM" MBdecay string indicating microbial decay, one  following ""LM" "DD"

what: The default inital carbon pool values the basic MEMC model configurations from Wang et al. 2013 — default_initial

title: The default inital carbon pool values the basic MEMC model configurations from Wang et al. 2013 — default_initial
The default inital carbon pool values the basic MEMC model configurations from Wang et al. 2013 — default_initial
text: vector intial states Carbon pools MEND 2013 Wang et al. 2013. 'P' 'Particulate organic carbon POC' 'M' 'Mineral associated organic carbon MOC, note excludes Q' 'Q' 'Adsorbed phase DOC' 'B' 'Microbial biomass carbon MBC' 'D' 'Dissolved organic carbon DOC' 'EP' 'Enzyme decomposition P' 'EM' 'Ensyme decomposition M' 'IC' 'Accumulated CO2 flux' 'Tot' 'Total carbon patch'

what: Fit a MEMC model to a comparison data — memc_modfit
title: Fit a MEMC model to a comparison data — memc_modfit
text: Fit MEMC model comparison data

title: Fit a MEMC model to a comparison data — memc_modfit
code: memc_modfit(config, x, comp_data, lower = -Inf, upper = Inf, ...)

title: Fit a MEMC model to a comparison data — memc_modfit
text: config memc model configuration x vector parameters state values fit using FME::modFit comp_data data frame containing comparison fit data lower lower bounds parameters; unbounded set equal -Inf upper bounds parameters; unbounded set equal Inf ... addition arguments may passed FME::modFit

title: Fit a MEMC model to a comparison data — memc_modfit
text: results FME::modFit

title: Solve a MEMC configuration — solve_model
